skill.name: pexoai-agent
skill.description: AI-powered short video creation agent. Submit a description and the Pexo backend handles scriptwriting, shot composition, transitions, and music. Supports product ads, social media clips, brand videos, explainers, and promotional videos.
skill.summary: Create professional short videos (5-120 seconds) by describing what you want. Pexo handles all creative production work.
skill.author: pexoai

security_audit.summary: All 534 static analysis findings are FALSE POSITIVES. This is a legitimate CLI tool that integrates with the Pexo AI video API. The external commands, network calls, filesystem access, and env var reads are all expected behaviors for a CLI tool that calls an authenticated REST API. No evidence of malicious intent, data exfiltration, credential theft, or obfuscation was found. The scripts are transparent, well-structured, and use safe coding patterns (set -euo pipefail, temp file cleanup, credential masking in output). Safe to publish with documentation about access patterns.

low_findings.title: Shell command execution in bash scripts
low_findings.description: 349 instances of shell commands (curl, jq, mktemp, sed, awk) flagged as external_commands. All are standard CLI tool operations with hardcoded or variable-controlled arguments. No user-supplied command injection vector exists. These are expected for a shell-based API client.

low_findings.title: Network calls to pexo.ai API
low_findings.description: 27 instances of hardcoded URLs and network operations. All URLs point to pexo.ai (the legitimate service being integrated) or its CDN (aliyuncs.com). No data exfiltration to unknown endpoints. Expected for an API-integrated tool.

low_findings.title: Filesystem access (config and temp files)
low_findings.description: 79 instances flagged for filesystem access. Scripts read ~/.pexo/config (own config file), create temp files via mktemp with proper cleanup, and access /dev/null for output suppression. All operations are transparent and expected for a CLI tool that needs configuration and temporary storage.

low_findings.title: Environment variable access for API credentials
low_findings.description: 25 instances of env_access flagged. Scripts access PEXO_API_KEY and PEXO_BASE_URL which are documented required environment variables. Credentials are used only for Bearer token authentication to pexo.ai API. The pexo-doctor.sh script includes a mask_secret function that safely masks the key when displaying it in output.

low_findings.title: Heuristic: Dangerous combination of code+network+credentials
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged the combination of shell execution, network calls, and credential access as suspicious. This combination is NECESSARY for any CLI tool that integrates with an authenticated REST API. All network calls target pexo.ai only. No evidence of exfiltration or misuse.

low_findings.title: False positive: Weak cryptographic algorithm
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged sk- prefix checks and timestamp commands as weak cryptographic algorithms. Scripts check for sk- prefix as basic API key format validation (not cryptography). Date +%s000 generates timestamps for API requests. Neither is a cryptographic operation.

low_findings.title: False positive: Windows SAM database reference
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged SKILL.md line 43 as referencing the Windows SAM database. Line 43 contains the text 'This is non-negotiable.' (part of the language matching instruction). No actual SAM database reference exists. Static analyzer pattern-matching error.

content.user_title: Create Short AI Videos by Describing Them
content.value_statement: Making professional short videos requires expensive tools and creative skills. This skill connects you to the Pexo AI agent that handles all video production - scriptwriting, shot composition, transitions, and music - so you can create product ads, social clips, and brand videos just by describing what you want.
actual_capabilities: Creates short videos (5-120 seconds) from a text description
Uploads and references images, videos, and audio files as source material
Polls for video production progress and delivers the final file and URL
Handles revisions by sending new feedback to the existing project
Provides preview options when the AI generates multiple video versions
limitations: Video production takes approximately 15-20 minutes for a short video
Cannot crawl web URLs - files must be downloaded and uploaded first
Requires an active Pexo account with API key and available credits
Only supports output up to 120 seconds in length

use_cases.title: Marketing teams creating product videos
use_cases.description: A marketer describes a new product and its key features. The Pexo agent creates a promotional video with professional transitions and music.

use_cases.title: Content creators making social media clips
use_cases.description: A TikTok or Instagram creator describes a short video idea. The AI handles the creative work and delivers a platform-ready clip.

use_cases.title: Businesses producing explainer videos
use_cases.description: A business owner describes their service and target audience. The AI generates an explainer video with appropriate visuals and pacing.

prompt_templates.title: Simple video request
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 30-second product video showing our new running shoes. Focus on comfort and durability. Use energetic music.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time use - describe a video in natural language

prompt_templates.title: Video with uploaded reference image
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a photo of our new coffee packaging. Create a 15-second brand video that introduces this product to the market.
prompt_templates.scenario: Video based on a product photo

prompt_templates.title: Video revision with feedback
prompt_templates.prompt: The previous video is good but please make the pacing slower in the first 5 seconds. Add more emphasis on the eco-friendly materials.
prompt_templates.scenario: Requesting changes to an existing video

prompt_templates.title: Multi-asset video with audio
prompt_templates.prompt: I have uploaded three product photos and a background music track. Create a 45-second brand story video that showcases our company values.

output_examples.input: Create a 20-second ad for our new mobile app. Show someone using the app in daily life.
output_examples.output: [A 20-second video is produced showing a person using the mobile app in various daily scenarios - at a coffee shop, on the subway, and at home. The video includes transitions and background music. The download URL and project page are shared with the user.]

output_examples.input: Make a 15-second brand video for my bakery using this photo of our storefront.
output_examples.output: [After uploading the storefront photo, a 15-second brand video is created featuring the bakery exterior, animated text overlay with the bakery name, and warm-toned visuals. The final video file and URL are delivered to the user.]

output_examples.input: Create a 60-second instructional video about how our software works.
output_examples.output: [Pexo may respond with clarifying questions about the target audience and key features to highlight. After receiving answers, a 60-second instructional video is produced. The project page link and download URL are shared.]

best_practices: Copy the user exact words when sending to Pexo. Do not add creative details the user did not mention. Pexo has professional video expertise that produces better results when given the original request.
Upload files before referencing them in messages. Always wrap asset IDs in the correct tags: original-image, original-video, or original-audio. Bare asset IDs are ignored.
Consolidate all requirements into one message when possible. Each message to Pexo costs tokens, so combining feedback into a single message is more efficient.
anti_patterns: Do not add duration, aspect ratio, style descriptions, or other production details the user did not mention. This overrides Pexo professional judgment and produces worse videos.
Do not send multiple separate messages when one message would work. Each message triggers processing and consumes credits.
Do not use markdown link formatting for video URLs. Send the full URL as plain text including all query parameters. Markdown wrapping or truncated URLs result in 403 errors.

faq.question: What video lengths does Pexo support?
faq.answer: Pexo creates short videos between 5 and 120 seconds. The exact length depends on your requirements and the complexity of the content.

faq.question: What aspect ratios are available?
faq.answer: Pexo supports three aspect ratios: 16:9 (widescreen), 9:16 (vertical for mobile), and 1:1 (square).

faq.question: How long does video production take?
faq.answer: A 15-second video typically takes 15-20 minutes. Longer or more complex videos may take more time. You can track progress through the project page.

faq.question: What file formats can I upload?
faq.answer: Images: jpg, png, webp, bmp, tiff, heic. Videos: mp4, mov, avi. Audio: mp3, wav, aac, m4a, ogg, flac.

faq.question: How do I get a Pexo API key?
faq.answer: Create an account at pexo.ai, go to the API keys section, and generate a new key. The key starts with sk- prefix. Set it as the PEXO_API_KEY environment variable or add it to ~/.pexo/config.

faq.question: Can I revise an existing video?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the same project ID and send your feedback as a new message. The Pexo agent will process the revision using the existing project context.
